name: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: One of the world's largest and most comprehensive art museums. The Met's collection spans 5,000 years and includes masterpieces from every major movement in Western art, from ancient Egypt to contemporary installations.

name: Museum of Modern Art (MoMA)
description: A defining institution for modern and contemporary art, MoMA's collection includes iconic works from Impressionism through today. Its galleries house Van Gogh's Starry Night, Picasso's Les Demoiselles d'Avignon, and Monet's Water Lilies.

name: Solomon R. Guggenheim Museum
description: Housed in Frank Lloyd Wright's iconic spiral building, the Guggenheim holds an outstanding collection of Impressionist, Post-Impressionist, early Modern European, and contemporary artworks.

name: Whitney Museum of American Art
description: Dedicated to 20th- and 21st-century American art, the Whitney features paintings, sculptures, and new media by American artists, with a strong focus on living artists and contemporary movements.

name: Neue Galerie New York
description: Devoted to early twentieth-century German and Austrian art and design. The collection includes major works by Klimt, Schiele, Kandinsky, and other Expressionists.

name: The Frick Collection
description: An intimate museum in a Gilded Age mansion housing Old Master paintings, European sculpture, and decorative arts. Highlights include works by Vermeer, Rembrandt, Bellini, and Turner.

name: Musée du Louvre
description: The world's largest art museum and historic monument in Paris. Home to the Mona Lisa, Venus de Milo, and an unparalleled collection spanning antiquity to the mid-19th century.

name: Musée d'Orsay
description: Housed in a former railway station in Paris, the Musée d'Orsay holds the world's foremost collection of Impressionist and Post-Impressionist masterpieces, alongside Realist and Art Nouveau works.
address: 1 Rue de la Légion d'Honneur, 75007 Paris, France

name: Museo del Prado
description: Spain's national museum in Madrid, renowned for its collection of European art from the 12th to early 20th century, with masterpieces by Goya, Velázquez, El Greco, and Bosch.
address: Calle de Ruiz de Alarcón 23, 28014 Madrid, Spain

name: The National Gallery
description: Located in Trafalgar Square, London, the National Gallery houses over 2,300 Western European paintings from the mid-13th century to 1900, including works by Turner, Constable, and the Impressionists.

name: Art Institute of Chicago
description: One of the oldest and largest art museums in the United States. It is renowned for its Impressionist and Post-Impressionist collection, including Seurat's A Sunday on La Grande Jatte and Grant Wood's American Gothic.

name: San Francisco Museum of Modern Art (SFMOMA)
description: One of the largest museums of modern and contemporary art in the United States. Its collection includes masterpieces by Henri Matisse, Frida Kahlo, and Andy Warhol.

name: Albright-Knox Art Gallery
description: Located in Buffalo, New York, the Albright-Knox is one of the oldest public arts institutions in the US, known for its exceptional collection of modern and contemporary art, including Balla's Dynamism of a Dog on a Leash.

name: Minneapolis Institute of Art
description: A major art museum in Minneapolis, home to more than 90,000 works of art representing 5,000 years of world history. It houses Poussin's Death of Germanicus.

name: National Gallery of Norway
description: Part of the National Museum in Oslo, it holds Norway's largest collection of paintings, drawings, and sculptures, including Edvard Munch's The Scream.

name: Museo Nacional Centro de Arte Reina Sofía
description: Spain's national museum of 20th-century art, located in Madrid. It is home to Picasso's anti-war masterpiece Guernica and significant works by Dalí and Miró.
address: Calle de Santa Isabel 52, 28012 Madrid, Spain

name: State Tretyakov Gallery
description: The foremost depository of Russian fine art in the world, located in Moscow. Its collection includes Kandinsky's Composition VII and iconic works from the Russian avant-garde.

name: Leopold Museum
description: Located in the Museumsquartier in Vienna, it houses one of the world's most important collections of modern Austrian art, featuring the largest Egon Schiele collection.

name: National Galleries of Scotland
description: Scotland's national collection of fine art, housed in several prominent buildings in Edinburgh. It includes Gauguin's Vision After the Sermon.

name: Villa Albani
description: A villa in Rome built for Cardinal Alessandro Albani to house his collection of antiquities and art. It is famous for Mengs's Parnassus ceiling fresco.

name: Various Collections
description: Items in private collections or distributed across various institutions around the world.

name: Art Gallery of Ontario (AGO)
description: Located in Toronto, the AGO is one of the largest art museums in North America. Its collection includes over 90,000 works of art, with a strong focus on Canadian art and the Henry Moore Sculpture Centre.
address: 317 Dundas Street West, Toronto, ON M5T 1G4, Canada

name: Hamburger Kunsthalle
description: One of the largest art museums in Germany, focusing on European art from the Middle Ages to the present day. It is famous for Caspar David Friedrich's Wanderer Above the Sea of Fog.

name: National Gallery of Canada
description: Canada's national art museum, located in Ottawa. It houses a comprehensive collection of Canadian, Indigenous, and European art, including West's Death of General Wolfe.
address: 380 Sussex Drive, Ottawa, ON K1N 9N4, Canada
